threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch every time a bus zooms past—vets say it takes three months to join the Dalston dodge.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and overheard two blokes debating whether a chicken shop ever beats a kebab stand. This is the real South London dilemma.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted an impromptu dance party in the U-Bahn while waiting for yet another delayed train on a Monday morning.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do buses have their own breaks for strikes. You’ll see them parked, but the tram just keeps rolling, like nothing's wrong.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they instinctively cross 16th St. when they hear the BART announcement, dodging the hustle like it’s second nature.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a tuxedo giving his date a tour of the bus stops like they were art installations. Only in SF can transit be a romantic backdrop.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists love the parks, but they miss the real vibe in the plaça during evening closures—locals gather, swap stories, and share meals.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the heavy backpacks filled with guidebooks; locals know the best spots aren't in the brochures.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'train' station instead of a 'tube' station—everyone's giving you side-eye on the platform.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and saw a bloke trying to barter with the driver for a fare. Only in South London, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using a broken U-Bahn bench as a stage for their rap battle outside the Karstadt. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bar disguised as a laundromat—grab a drink while your clothes spin. It's a weirdly comforting vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they can spot a tourist from a block away just by the way they hold their phone, snapping pics of every mural.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo hop on the 19 Polk after a wedding, only to slip into a deep conversation about the meaning of life with a busstop philosopher.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just another trendy spot. Locals know the secret: it's the voices of the older residents that echo the real soul of the neighborhood.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their bewilderment over the APCs—advanced public computers—at most metro stations, they always fumble with tickets.
